evals.name: register-new-summarize-tool
evals.prompt: I'm working in an MCP server codebase that uses @modelcontextprotocol/server v2 and zod/v4. Add a new tool called `summarize` that takes a `text` field (string, min 1 char) and an optional `maxWords` field (integer 1-500, default 100), and returns a structured object with `summary` (string) and `wordCount` (integer). For now just stub the implementation by returning the first N words of the input. Make it follow v2 best practices. Show me the complete tool file as I would save it under `src/tools/summarize.ts`.
evals.expected_output: A complete summarize.ts file using registerTool, z.strictObject for both inputSchema and outputSchema, .describe() on every field, structuredContent in the result, content array also present, proper imports from @modelcontextprotocol/server and zod/v4, .js extension on relative imports, no console.log.

evals.name: migrate-v1-server-to-v2
evals.expected_output: Migrated file with: imports from @modelcontextprotocol/server, @modelcontextprotocol/node, @modelcontextprotocol/express; zod/v4 import; registerTool() (not .tool()); inputSchema as z.strictObject; ctx.mcpReq.signal/notify (not extra.signal/sendNotification); ProtocolError (not McpError); setRequestHandler with string 'tools/list'; NodeStreamableHTTPServerTransport; createMcpExpressApp.

evals.name: minimal-stdio-client
evals.prompt: Build me a minimal MCP client in TypeScript that uses @modelcontextprotocol/client v2 to spawn a local stdio MCP server (npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything), connect to it, list all available tools (paginated), call the first tool that has no required arguments, and print both the structuredContent and the text content. Handle errors properly — both tool-level errors and protocol/SDK errors. Make it work cross-platform (Windows + macOS + Linux). Show the full file.
evals.expected_output: Complete client file with: Client + StdioClientTransport import from @modelcontextprotocol/client; cross-platform npx (npx.cmd on win32); pagination loop over listTools with cursor; try/catch handling both isError result branch AND ProtocolError + SdkError thrown errors; transport.stderr piped; cleanup with client.close() in finally.

evals.name: add-progress-and-timeouts
evals.expected_output: Updated tool that: adds ctx as second handler parameter (async (args, ctx) => ...); reads ctx.mcpReq._meta?.progressToken and only emits progress when it exists; uses ctx.mcpReq.notify with method 'notifications/progress' and params {progressToken, progress, total, message}; passes ctx.mcpReq.signal to fetch (signal: ctx.mcpReq.signal) so it aborts; returns {content, isError: true} for upstream HTTP failures (does NOT throw); does not throw ProtocolError for normal upstream failures; preserves outputSchema/structuredContent contract.
